topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The unexpected bouquet landed on my desk with a soft, unsettling thud. Crimson roses, impossibly vibrant against the sterile grey of my workspace. I stared at them, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. Who sends these? There was no note, just the fragrant, undeniable presence of them. I picked one up, the velvety petals cool against my palm, and immediately dropped it back onto the polished wood. My hands were clenched so tightly my knuckles were white, and I could feel a slow, hot burn creeping up my neck. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the floor, and walked toward the door, needing distance from the sheer, baffling intrusion.

[story 2]

I found the arrangement tucked into the corner of my otherwise untouched apartment. A cascade of pale lilies, dripping with dew, sat beside a small, unmarked vase. A knot formed in my stomach, a tight, unpleasant clenching. Why? It felt like a deliberate provocation. I circled the vase slowly, my gaze sharp and assessing, cataloging every detail, searching for some hidden meaning, some veiled insult. When I finally reached out to touch a petal, my fingers recoiled as if burned. I snatched my hand back, breathing in a shallow, quick gasp, my posture stiffening into an unyielding line.

[story 3]

The flowers appeared on my doorstep just as I was finishing a particularly frustrating phone call. They were sunflowers, aggressively cheerful in a way that grated on my nerves. I didn't move for a full minute, just stood there, rigid, the tension radiating off me like heat. I could feel the pulse hammering against my temples. I finally bent down, my movements slow and deliberate, and picked up the stems. The weight of them felt significant, an imposition. I didn't look toward the street; I focused only on the task of putting them down, my expression set in a mask of profound, cold indifference, as if accepting this unsolicited gesture was the most tiresome thing I had ever had to endure.
